title: taraxacum rumelicum
intro: Taraxacum rumelicum, also known as Macedonian dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve liver and kidney problems. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Taraxacum rumelicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for promoting overall health and well-being.
